title: Inner Sect Rules
content:
Life in the Inner Sect was far different from the Outer Sect. The rules were stricter, the competition fiercer, and the expectations higher.

"Listen up," Elder Wu said, standing in front of the class. "In the Inner Sect, only the strong survive. You have three years to reach the Foundation Establishment realm. If you fail, you'll be sent back to the Outer Sect. And if you fail there, you'll be expelled from the sect entirely."

The disciples shifted nervously. Foundation Establishment was no easy feat - many spent decades trying to reach that level.

"But don't worry," Elder Wu said, a faint smile on his face. "If you work hard, you might just make it."

Chen Fan listened carefully. In his past life, he had reached Foundation Establishment at the age of twenty, which was considered fast. But this time, with his past knowledge, he could do it much sooner.

The next few weeks were grueling. The Inner Sect disciples trained from dawn till dusk, pushing their bodies to the limit. Chen Fan kept up easily, but he noticed that many of his fellow disciples were struggling.

"This is too hard," one disciple complained. "I can't keep up."

"Quit if you want," another said. "No one's forcing you to stay."

Chen Fan watched the exchange, his mind racing. The Inner Sect was a place of ruthless competition, and he needed to be careful.

One day, after training, Chen Fan was approached by a group of disciples led by Zhou Tianming.

"Chen Fan," Zhou Tianming said, stepping forward. "I have a proposition for you."

"What is it?"

"Join us," Zhou Tianming said. "We're forming a group to help each other train. With your skills and our resources, we could all reach Foundation Establishment in record time."

Chen Fan studied Zhou Tianming's face. He could see the ambition in his eyes, but there was also something else - greed.

"What's the catch?" Chen Fan asked.

"No catch," Zhou Tianming said. "Just teamwork."

Chen Fan shook his head. "I prefer to train alone."

Zhou Tianming's smile faded. "Suit yourself," he said. "But don't come crying to me when you're stuck at the bottom."

He turned and walked away, the other disciples following him.

Chen Fan watched them go, a sense of unease settling in his chest. Zhou Tianming wasn't just trying to recruit him - he was trying to control him.

***

That night, Chen Fan went to visit Wang Tieshan, who was still in the Outer Sect.

"How's the Inner Sect?" Wang Tieshan asked, handing Chen Fan a bowl of noodles.

"It's... different," Chen Fan said. "The competition is fierce, and everyone's out for themselves."

"That sounds terrible," Wang Tieshan said. "Why did you agree to go?"

"The Sect Master offered me a direct promotion," Chen Fan said. "I couldn't refuse."

"I see," Wang Tieshan said. "Well, if you ever need help, just let me know. I'll always have your back."

Chen Fan smiled. Wang Tieshan was one of the few people he could trust.

As he walked back to the Inner Sect, Chen Fan noticed something strange. A shadowy figure was following him.

He quickened his pace, but the figure kept up. Finally, he turned around.

"Who are you?"

The figure stepped into the light. It was Lin Xue.

"I'm sorry," she said. "I didn't mean to scare you. I just wanted to talk."

"What about?"

"I wanted to apologize," Lin Xue said. "For earlier. I was being too forward."

"It's okay," Chen Fan said.

"No, it's not," Lin Xue said. "I know you probably think I'm just some spoiled rich girl who's used to getting what she wants. But that's not who I am."

She looked at him, her eyes earnest.

"I want to be your friend, Chen Fan. Not because of your reputation or your skills, but because I think you're a good person."

Chen Fan hesitated. Should he trust her? In his past life, she had betrayed him. But this Lin Xue was different - younger, more sincere.

"Okay," he said. "We can be friends."

Lin Xue smiled, and Chen Fan felt his heart skip a beat.

He quickly shook off the feeling. He couldn't let his guard down around her.

***

The next day, Chen Fan received a message from the Sect Master. He was to report to the Sect Master's hall immediately.

When he arrived, the Sect Master was waiting for him, along with several other elders.

"Chen Fan," the Sect Master said. "We have an important mission for you."

"What is it?"

"The Black Wind Gang has been causing trouble on the border," the Sect Master said. "They've been robbing merchants and attacking our patrols. We need someone to investigate."

"Why me?"

"Because you're the most promising young disciple we have," the Sect Master said. "And this mission will test your skills."

Chen Fan nodded. "I'll go."

"Good," the Sect Master said. "Take Wang Tieshan with you. He's strong, and he'll be a good companion."

Chen Fan thanked the Sect Master and left. As he walked back to his room, he couldn't help but wonder if this mission was really just a test.

Or was it something more?

He would find out soon enough.
